conclusion: Current c8 rowchunk2/selected-c1 512/128 retained-bench path now attaches the combined c2/c4/c8 projection-dispatch catalog and selects benchmark_accepted_caware_projection. The final retained artifact has generated-token equality [137,137,137,137,137,137,137,137], primitive correctness loaded/passed, profiler evidence captured, and projection-dispatch blockers absent. This is not a throughput claim: c8 remains blocked by rowchunked full attention/native_caware_decode=false and selected-c1 MoE retained metadata, and the observed rerun instability should be watched before promotion.
remaining_blockers: c8 full attention still uses native_batch_row_chunks and native_caware_decode=false
selected-c1 batch MoE remains a retained metadata blocker (grouped_compact required for retained MoE claim)
c8 projection-dispatch repeatability needs more evidence before any promotion beyond correctness artifacts
scaling ratios are attached but not used for a throughput claim while correctness/perf eligibility blockers remain
repeatability_note: During this iteration, earlier same-command retained reruns with the c8 projection catalog produced red prefix vectors before a final path/provenance-matched rerun returned full equality. Keep c8 projection-dispatch repeatability under observation; no throughput claim is made.

batch_blockers: native c>N decode currently supports compact physical-slot-ordered rows; full-attention batch context is native only for BF16 KV and context < 1024
full-attention decode forced to native row-chunk diagnostic path
decision_reason_summary: execution.batch_execution.row_execution must not contain serial or fallback
execution.batch_execution.decode_execution.moe_decode_path must be grouped_compact for retained c>N MoE decode
decode_blockers: full-attention decode forced to native row-chunk diagnostic path
